rows.item: 学習意欲・授業参加度

summary: This course will help students build their spoken communication skills in English. It will introduce the conversation skills for asking questions, responding to questions, expressing one's opinion, and negotiating misunderstandings. Students will have frequent opportunities to communicate with their classmates in English and practice the grammar, vocabulary, and techniques they learn in class.
aims: This is a speaking-focused English language course. The course will introduce a variety of practical skills for expressing oneself and conversing with others in English. In addition to skill building, the aim is that the course will develop speaking confidence and assist students in putting their overall knowledge of English to use in conversation.
goals: Gain practice and confidence in speaking English

plan.text:
[Course overview]
Introduction and overview of the course, assessments and other requirements. Meet your teacher and classmates.

[Follow-up questions]
Questions we can use to keep a conversation going.

[Understanding each other]
Skills for staying in English during conversation

[Agreeing and disagreeing]
Language for expressing your opinion

[Comments]
Short responses that show that you understood your partner.
[Final group speaking test introduction and practice]

[Final group speaking test]
Students will complete the group speaking test in two groups. A group will take the test. B group will review the conversation techniques we learned during this course and reflect on participation.

[Final group speaking test]
Students will complete the group speaking test in two groups. B group will take the test. A group will review the conversation techniques we learned during this course and reflect on participation.

textbooks: Materials will be introduced/provided in class
prereq:
-Students should be willing to participate in class activities including spoken activities, group work, etc.
-Students must submit all assessments, including spoken presentations, essays, blog posts, etc., before the end of the last lesson (unless there is a written agreement with the teacher stating that the student can submit at a later date). If all assessment tasks are not completed, the student will not be able to pass the course.
-As this is a practice-based course, students must be absent no more than three times.
prep:
It is necessary to preview lesson content before class and review after class.
Students should complete any homework assigned in class.
teachers: ◎ マガシック ミケランジェロ

extra.label: 区分1
extra.text: 平成20年度以降入学生／令和６年度以降入学生

extra.label: 区分2
extra.text: 共通教育）初年次科目／国際コミュニケーション科目基軸英語

extra.label: 履修における注意点
extra.text: １年次に必修。１外国語（英語）履修上限年間10単位。卒業にはⅠとⅡ両方が必要。

extra.label: 資格等
extra.text: 教免－省令で定める科目：外国語コミュニケーション

extra.label: この授業で身につける「10+1の能力」
extra.text:
語学に関するリテラシー
コミュニケーション力
協働実践力

extra.label: 成績評価に関する補足
extra.text:
Participation (40) Attitude and attendance, completing work, bringing your textbook to class, answering questions, and speaking in English during conversation activities.
Midterm: Interactive presentation (20) Give a short presentation in a small group and answer questions. Ask questions to other group members about their presentations.
Final: Group speaking test (40) 3 people, 8 minutes, free conversation, random groups. Scored following the criteria outlined in the Eikaiwa handbook.

extra.label: オフィスアワーに関する補足
教員の実務経験の有無
extra.text:
有
教員の実務経験との関連

extra.label: 授業形態
extra.text:
その他
Teacher introduces the lesson content. Students are expected to answer the teacher's questions and/or demonstrate that they know the vocabulary for each unit.
Students participate in pair conversation for approximately half of the lesson duration (45 mins).
